stemHtml: What is the primary method used to forecast turbulence?

options.a: Turbulence is forecasted by models that estimate turbulence based on associated situations unless a pilot report indicates turbulence in the area.
options.b: Significant weather charts that always show the most up-to-date information about turbulence.
options.c: Relying on visual observations made by ground weather stations.
options.d: The pilot reports only when reporting light turbulence.
